module.name: require-barrel-relative-exports
module.description: Require barrel re-export declarations to use current-directory descendant paths that start with './'.

scenarios.name: should ignore non-barrel files
scenarios.given: a non-index source file containing a parent re-export declaration

scenarios.name: should allow named and wildcard re-exports from current-directory descendants
scenarios.given: an index.ts file containing export { foo } from './foo' and export * from './feature/utils'
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the barrel re-export declarations

scenarios.name: should allow type-only and namespace re-exports from current-directory descendants
scenarios.given: an index.ts file containing export type { Foo } from './types/foo' and export * as utils from './utils'
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the barrel re-export declarations

scenarios.name: should ignore local export declarations with no source
scenarios.given: an index.ts file containing export { foo } with no from source
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the export declaration
scenarios.then: no violation is reported because only re-export declarations are checked

scenarios.name: should apply to single-extension JavaScript barrel files
scenarios.given: an index.js file containing export { foo } from './foo'
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the barrel re-export declaration

scenarios.name: should ignore double-extension index files
scenarios.given: an index.test.ts or index.d.ts file containing a non-relative re-export declaration
scenarios.then: no violation is reported because double-extension files are not treated as barrels

scenarios.name: should report parent re-export paths in barrel files
scenarios.given: an index.ts file containing export { foo } from '../parent' or export * from '..'
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the barrel re-export declarations
scenarios.then: a relativeBarrelExport violation is reported for each non-relative path

scenarios.name: should report package, aliased, and absolute re-export paths in barrel files
scenarios.given: an index.ts file containing export { foo } from 'package-name', export type { Foo } from '@/types', or export * as ns from '/root/value'
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the barrel re-export declarations
scenarios.then: a relativeBarrelExport violation is reported for each path that does not start with './'

scenarios.name: should fix verified bare same-directory re-export paths
scenarios.given: an index.ts file containing export { foo } from 'foo' where a sibling foo module exists
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the barrel re-export declaration with a real filename
scenarios.then: a relativeBarrelExport violation is reported with a fix to rewrite the path to './foo'

scenarios.name: should skip fix output when a re-export source is unavailable
scenarios.given: a fix callback is evaluated for a re-export node without a source

scenarios.name: should report the bare './' token in barrel files
scenarios.given: an index.ts file containing export * from './'
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the barrel re-export declaration
scenarios.then: a relativeBarrelExport violation is reported because the path does not target a descendant module

scenarios.name: should report current-directory paths that traverse to a parent directory
scenarios.given: an index.ts file containing export { foo } from './../foo' or export * from './..'
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the barrel re-export declarations
scenarios.then: a relativeBarrelExport violation is reported because './' paths containing '..' segments do not remain within the descendant tree
